title: University or Trade School: Which is Best for You?
passage:
University or Trade School: Which is Best for You?

Choosing what to do after high school is a very big decision for young people. Many students choose to go to university, which is also called higher education. Other students choose vocational training, which is also called trade school. Both choices are good, but they are very different in many ways.

University is usually for four years. Students at university study subjects like history, science, or business. They read many big books and write long papers. Students at university learn a lot of theory. After they finish their studies, they get a university degree. With this degree, they can get jobs like school teachers, office managers, or scientists. However, university can be very expensive. Students often need to pay a lot of money for tuition every year.

Vocational training is different. It is much shorter, usually taking only one or two years. Students learn practical skills for specific jobs. For example, they learn how to repair cars, build houses, cook food, or style hair. They spend most of their time doing hands-on work in workshops, not reading books. After they finish, they get a certificate. These jobs are always needed, so students can find a job very quickly. Vocational schools are also much cheaper than universities.

Salaries can also be different. People with university degrees often earn more money later in life. However, people who do vocational training start earning money much earlier because they finish school quickly.

In conclusion, both university and vocational training are excellent paths. University is good for people who love studying books and want office jobs. Vocational training is great for people who like to work with their hands and want to start working quickly. Every student should choose the path that is best for their future.

vocabulary.meaning: education at a college or university level
education and training that teaches you the skills you need for a specific job
a qualification given to student who finishes university
an official document proving that you completed a course of study or training
relating to real action and experience rather than theories

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Với trình độ A1-A2, câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy định vị từ khóa trong câu hỏi và quét (scan) nhanh trong bài đọc để tìm đoạn chứa từ khóa đó.

2. Không tự suy luận: Tránh đoán nghĩa sâu xa hoặc tự suy diễn. Đáp án luôn nằm trực tiếp ngay trên mặt chữ của bài đọc.

3. Giới hạn từ: Chú ý yêu cầu số lượng từ (ví dụ: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Nếu bạn viết 3 từ, câu trả lời của bạn sẽ bị tính là sai cho dù thông tin có đúng.
